name: Qwen3.6-Plus
archDisplay: Hosted product tier of the Qwen3.6 hybrid-attention line (Gated DeltaNet + Gated Attention, Qwen3-Next lineage); exact parameter/expert/MoE configuration for the hosted Plus checkpoint undisclosed
note: Flagship hosted model of the Qwen3.6 generation ('Towards Real World Agents'); massive agentic-coding upgrade over Qwen3.5-Plus per the launch blog, with a 1M-token default context and improved multimodal perception/reasoning. This is the model matching the leads' 'Qwen 3.6 Plus' entry; a second, separately-dated lead for the same product name was found to be a duplicate (see excluded).

results.notes: inferred as the Qwen3.6-Plus column (rightmost/newest of 5 unlabeled columns in the launch blog's comparison table, consistent with this being the announcement post for that exact model)
GPQA; same column-position inference as above
LiveCodeBench v6; same column-position inference
AIME 2026 (I & II); same column-position inference

SWE-bench Verified, resolved rate. Attributed specifically to the hosted Qwen3.6-Plus tier (not the open-weight Qwen3.6-35B-A3B, which independently scores a distinctly lower 73.4% per a DEV Community writeup of the HF release -- good disambiguation signal against variant-conflation). Corroborated by a second aggregator, benchlm.ai (https://benchlm.ai/models/qwen3-6-plus, same figure, tagged 'Provider source'), and loosely by a third-party social post. Could not verify against Alibaba's own primary blog because its comparison table is embedded as images, unreadable via text fetch. Confidence: moderate -- two aggregators agree and the number is well clear of the smaller sibling model's score, but neither aggregator is the primary source.

Terminal-Bench 2.0, agentic terminal-use eval. Value 61.6% corroborated across four independent write-ups that each summarize the vendor's launch table (tokenmix.ai, apidog.com, renovateqr.com, benchlm.ai) -- unusually strong agreement for an aggregator-sourced number. tokenmix.ai frames it as the metric where Qwen3.6-Plus 'genuinely leads', beating Claude Opus 4.5's 58.4%. Could not confirm directly against the primary qwen.ai/Alibaba Cloud blog, whose comparison table is image-embedded and unreadable via text fetch.

SWE-bench Pro (harder, contamination-resistant successor to SWE-bench Verified). 56.6% corroborated by three independent write-ups (apidog.com, renovateqr.com, benchlm.ai) all citing the same figure alongside the already-verified SWE-bench Verified 78.8% score, which strengthens confidence these aggregators are drawing from the vendor's real launch table rather than fabricating. Named explicitly in the primary Alibaba Cloud blog mirror's benchmark list ('SWE-bench Pro') but its numeric score is only visible in an embedded image there.

IFEval (strict), instruction-following. 94.3% corroborated by a second independent aggregator, benchlm.ai, with an identical figure. Distinct from the separately-tracked IFBench score (75.2%) added below -- the two are different benchmarks with different, non-conflicting scores, consistent with them being genuinely different evals rather than a mislabeled duplicate.

GPQA Diamond as independently measured by Artificial Analysis (distinct methodology/harness from the vendor's own self-reported 90.4% already in this file, hence recorded as a separate result). 88.2% corroborated exactly by a second aggregator, benchlm.ai ('AA-GPQA Diamond: 88.2%'). Could not load Artificial Analysis's own model page as text (JS-rendered dashboard) to confirm at the primary source.

Humanity's Last Exam, as measured by Artificial Analysis. 25.7% corroborated exactly by a second aggregator, benchlm.ai ('AA-HLE: 25.7%'). Not the vendor's own self-reported HLE figure (which the primary blog names as 'HLE w/ tool' / 'HLE-Verified' but does not expose numerically outside embedded images) -- this is the independent AA measurement.
IFBench, as measured by Artificial Analysis. 75.2% corroborated exactly by a second aggregator, benchlm.ai ('AA-IFBench: 75.2%'). Distinct from the IFEval result above.
tau2-Bench Telecom (agentic customer-support/tool-use eval), as measured by Artificial Analysis. 97.7% corroborated exactly by a second aggregator, benchlm.ai ('Tau2-Telecom: 97.7%').
Knowledge table, MMLU-Redux row.
Knowledge table, SuperGPQA row.
Knowledge table, C-Eval row.
Multilingualism table, MMMLU row.
Vision-language table, STEM and Puzzle section, MMMU row.
Vision-language table, MMMU-Pro row.
Vision-language table, MathVision row; Qwen notes the score uses a fixed 'reason step by step, \boxed{}' prompt, taking the higher of boxed/non-boxed for other models.
Vision-language table, Text Recognition and Document Understanding section, AI2D_TEST row.
Vision-language table, Spatial Intelligence section, 'RefCOCO(avg)' row (average across val/testA/testB splits).

Vision-language table, 'VideoMME(w sub.)' (with-subtitles) row. Used the with-subtitles variant to stay consistent with how the sibling Qwen3.6 corpus entries (qwen3-6-27b, qwen3-6-35b-a3b) report VideoMME from their own HF model cards.
Vision-language table, Visual Agent section, OSWorld-Verified row.
Vision-language table, 'CharXiv(RQ)' (reasoning-question subset) row -- matches this corpus's existing convention of using CharXiv's reasoning subset for the 'charxiv' slug.

Qwen3.6-Plus launch blog, 'Coding Agent' table. Internal agent scaffold (bash+file-edit tools), temp=1.0, top_p=0.95, 200K context; Qwen states they corrected some problematic tasks in the public SWE-bench Pro set and re-evaluated all baselines on the refined set.
Terminal-Bench 2.0; Harbor/Terminus-2 harness, 3h timeout, 32 CPU/48GB RAM, avg of 5 runs.

Humanity's Last Exam, no-tool baseline configuration (STEM & Reasoning table). The same blog also reports a distinct tool-augmented 'HLE w/ tool' score of 50.6 under 256K context with context-folding; that figure was intentionally NOT added here to avoid two conflicting scores under the single 'hle' taxonomy slug.
IFEval, strict-prompt setting (Instruction Following table).
Instruction Following table, IFBench row. [score corrected during round-5 full-corpus accuracy re-verify: 74.2 -> 75.8]
Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), OpenClaw harness, High reasoning effort: Pass rate 8.6% / Score 24.3% (rank 46). Score component used per corpus convention.

GDPval-AA v2 (Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index v4.1 component), normalized to the corpus's existing (Elo-500)/2000 convention (matches how the sibling Qwen3.7-Max record maps this same slug). benchlm.ai lists both the raw AA Elo-style score (1135) and a normalized figure (31.8%); the two are internally consistent -- (1135-500)/2000 = 31.75% -- which corroborates that this specific figure is drawn from a real AA feed rather than benchlm's padding (the corpus's own verificationNotes on this file flag benchlm.ai as unreliable for several made-up-sounding benchmark names, but GDPval-AA is a real, independently-checkable AA metric and the arithmetic lines up exactly). Artificial Analysis's own qwen3-6-plus page confirms GDPval-AA v2 is one of its 9 Intelligence Index components for this model but the page itself is JS-rendered and did not yield a readable per-benchmark score via direct fetch, so benchlm.ai is the only textually-readable source -- flagging this as moderate rather than high confidence. [score corrected during round-6 completeness verify: proposed 31.8 -> 1135]

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real via direct fetch of the official qwen.ai launch blog and cross-checked against two independent trackers: llm-stats.com (GA 'Mar 31, 2026') and Artificial Analysis (GA 'April 2, 2026', Intelligence Index 40/#46 of 188 models, confirms text+image+video input, 1M context, $0.50/$3.00 pricing). Alibaba's own Model Studio pricing page lists the current snapshot as 'qwen3.6-plus-2026-04-02', matching Artificial Analysis exactly and used as the releaseDate here; the blog's own publish-date metadata (Apr 1) and llm-stats (Mar 31) are within the same ~3-day window, consistent with staged regional rollout rather than a distinct later release -- resolving the leads' second 'Qwen3.6-Plus (~2026-04-15)' entry as a duplicate of this same product (see excluded). CAVEAT: the launch blog's main comparison table lost its column-header row during content extraction (the underlying page is JS-rendered); the Qwen3.6-Plus column was inferred by position (rightmost of 5) and by cross-reference with the near-identical table structure in the Qwen3.6-27B blog (which does have labeled headers and follows the same 'baselines-then-newest-model-last' pattern). This inference is corroborated only loosely by independent review aggregators (e.g. a report of 78.8% SWE-bench Verified for Qwen3.6-Plus, which does not appear in the captured excerpt) -- flagged as a real but not fully column-certain attribution; if wrong, the true source model for these 4 specific scores would be one of the four baseline models shown in the same table (not a fabricated model).

SUPPLEMENTARY COVERAGE PASS: Fetched qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3.6 directly: it is a client-side-rendered page and the text-fetch tool retrieved essentially no content ("only contains the word 'Qwen'"). Also fetched the mirror at alibabacloud.com/blog/qwen3-6-plus-towards-real-world-agents_603005: this one DOES render as real text and confirms the model is genuine and names many benchmarks it was evaluated on (SWE-Bench series, Terminal-Bench 2.0, Claw-Eval, SkillsBench, NL2Repo, QwenClawBench, QwenWebBench, TAU3-Bench, VITA-Bench, MCPMark, MCP-Atlas, HLE, AIME, MMLU-ProX, WMT24++, MAXIFE, MathVision, V*, TIR-Bench) -- but every actual numeric score is embedded in images the tool cannot read, so I could not verify any specific figure directly against the primary source, including the 4 already in the corpus.

I fetched benchlm.ai's Qwen3.6-Plus page twice (two URL variants, both resolve to the same content) and it returned a suspiciously large, suspiciously uniform ~55-benchmark table. Several category/benchmark names in it do not correspond to any real, findable benchmark (e.g. 'Claw-Eval', 'QwenClaw Bench', 'Toolathlon', 'WideResearch', 'DeepPlanning', 'MCP Atlas', 'Gert Labs Composite Game', 'ResearchClawBench', 'MMAnswer Bench', 'NOVA-63', 'AI-Needle', 'AA-LCR', 'Critical Physics Tasks', 'AA-Omniscience') -- they read as plausible-sounding padding interleaved with genuine ones (Terminal-Bench 2.0, HLE, SuperGPQA, MMLU-Redux, FrontierMath, MMMU-Pro, CharXiv, IFBench, IFEval, C-Eval, etc., all of which ARE real). A page mixing verifiable and unverifiable-sounding entries in one uniform list is a red flag I could not resolve, since the underlying site is also JS-rendered and I only have the fetch tool's text summary of it, not a way to inspect raw HTML/DOM myself. That undermines confidence in every individual number on that page that I could not independently corroborate elsewhere -- which is most of them. I would not treat benchlm.ai as a reliable standalone 'confirmation' source; the task's premise that the original 4 scores were 'confirmed via' benchlm.ai should be treated with the same skepticism.

I also found live evidence of exactly the variant-conflation risk this corpus's own audit already flagged as its top systemic issue: public sources right now conflate Qwen3.6-Plus (proprietary, hosted) with Qwen3.6-35B-A3B (open-weight, HuggingFace) and even Qwen3.7-Max (a different later model), each with different, similar-looking scores circulating for overlapping benchmark names. The corpus already correctly keeps these as separate model files, which helped me screen out several benchlm.ai numbers I could not confidently pin to the Plus tier specifically.

Net result: only ONE additional benchmark result cleared my bar for 'grounded in a real source, corroborated by more than one independent source, and clearly attributable to Qwen3.6-Plus specifically rather than a sibling variant': SWE-bench Verified 78.8% -> mapped to this corpus's 'swe-bench' slug (confirmed that's the existing convention -- every current swe-bench.csv row is explicitly SWE-bench Verified). I checked plausible additional slugs the blog names suggest exist (Terminal-Bench 2.0 -> terminal-bench-2, HLE -> hle, FrontierMath tiers -> frontiermath-tier1-3/frontiermath-tier4, MMMU-Pro -> mmmu-pro, CharXiv -> charxiv, IFBench/IFEval, SuperGPQA -> super-gpqa, MMLU-Redux -> mmlu-redux, C-Eval -> c-eval) but could not get a second source to confirm any specific score for the Plus tier on any of them, and declined to report benchlm.ai-only numbers given the reliability concerns above. Recommend not adding those without either (a) a way to OCR/read the primary blog's embedded table images, or (b) a credible independent leaderboard (e.g. Epoch AI's own FrontierMath/HLE hub, vals.ai, or swebench.com) explicitly listing 'Qwen3.6-Plus' -- Epoch AI's FrontierMath hub pages are themselves JS-rendered dashboards that didn't yield readable model-level scores through the fetch tool either.

SECOND SUPPLEMENTARY PASS (coverage-completeness, 2026-07-14): Re-ran the same aggregator sweep with a wider net and a stricter 'two independently-styled sites must agree on the exact number' bar (rather than treating any single benchlm.ai figure as sufficient, per the padding concern above). This surfaced 7 more results that cleared the bar: terminal-bench-2 (61.6%, agreed by 4 sites: tokenmix.ai, apidog.com, renovateqr.com, benchlm.ai), swe-bench-pro (56.6%, agreed by 3: apidog.com, renovateqr.com, benchlm.ai), ifeval (94.3%, agreed by 2: apidog.com, benchlm.ai), and four Artificial-Analysis-methodology figures each agreed exactly between designforonline.com and benchlm.ai: gpqa/independent (88.2%, distinct from the vendor's self-reported 90.4%), hle (25.7%), ifbench (75.2%, distinct from ifeval), tau2-bench-telecom (97.7%). vals.ai and artificialanalysis.ai's own model page were also fetched but remain JS-rendered dashboards that the text-fetch tool cannot extract individual scores from (only an aggregate 'Intelligence Index: 40' renders as text) -- consistent with the first pass's finding, so they could not serve as a primary-source check, only as confirmation the model is tracked there at all. Benchmarks named in the primary blog but still unconfirmed by any second source (Claw-Eval, QwenClawBench, NL2Repo, TAU3-Bench, VITA-Bench, MCPMark/MCP-Atlas, WideSearch, C-Eval, SuperGPQA, MMLU-Redux, CharXiv, MMMU-Pro, MathVision, FrontierMath tiers) were left out, per the same 'omit rather than guess' rule.

THIRD SUPPLEMENTARY PASS (core-benchmark completeness + comparability re-check, 2026-07-16): Checked three MISSING core-benchmark gaps (mmlu, math, humaneval) against all three required source tiers. (1) Primary: neither the qwen.ai launch blog nor its readable alibabacloud.com mirror name "MMLU" (only "MMLU-ProX"), "MATH" (only MathVision/AIME), or "HumanEval" anywhere in the captured benchmark list (SWE-Bench series, Terminal-Bench 2.0, Claw-Eval, SkillsBench, NL2Repo, QwenClawBench, QwenWebBench, TAU3-Bench, VITA-Bench, MCPMark, MCP-Atlas, HLE, AIME, MMLU-ProX, WMT24++, MAXIFE, MathVision, V*, TIR-Bench). (2) Independent: fetched Artificial Analysis's own qwen3-6-plus page (Intelligence Index components: GDPval-AA v2, tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR -- no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval among the 9 components), designforonline.com's AA-sourced writeup (GPQA Diamond, HLE, SciCode, TerminalBench Hard, tau2-Bench, IFBench, LCR -- same absence), and llm-stats.com (no per-benchmark scores rendered, footer-only benchmark links). vals.ai has a "Qwen 3.6 Plus" page listing an "MMLU Pro" column among its tracked evals but every column including MMLU Pro renders "0.0%" -- an unscored placeholder state, not a real measurement, so unusable as evidence either way. (3) Sibling cross-reference: both same-generation open-weight siblings' official HF model cards (Qwen3.6-27B, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B) also omit mmlu, math, and humaneval entirely, reporting mmlu-pro/mmlu-redux and mathvista/aime26 in their place -- consistent within the family. Concluded this is a genuine, expected absence (the Qwen3.6 line has moved to MMLU-Pro/MMLU-Redux and AIME/MathVision successors, and to LiveCodeBench/SWE-bench in place of HumanEval, matching the broader pattern of frontier labs no longer publishing saturated legacy benchmarks); proposing omitted_no_evidence for all three rather than guessing.

Also re-checked the flagged terminal-bench-2=61.6 comparability concern (light re-verify per task instructions, since this class of flag was already investigated twice in the 2026-07-13 audit and found systematically false-positive -- see project memory dataset-audit-2026-07.md, which names "avg of 5 runs" as the canonical false-positive example of the audit tool's negation-blind "average of" string match). Fetched the primary alibabacloud.com mirror directly: its footnote reads verbatim "Terminal-Bench 2.0: Harbor/Terminus-2 harness; 3h timeout, 32 CPU/48 GB RAM; ...; avg of 5 runs" -- confirming the phrase in this file's own self-reported note ("avg of 5 runs") refers to averaging 5 repeated runs of Terminal-Bench 2.0 itself, not a blend with the separately-slugged "terminal-bench" v1 benchmark or any other eval. Independently re-checked all sources cited for the second (independent-source) terminal-bench-2 entry -- tokenmix.ai ("a single performance score... not an average or composite"), apidog.com ("standalone score... not a blended result from multiple benchmarks"), and benchlm.ai (single row, "Provider exact" provenance, weighted 38% into the Agentic category but 61.6% itself is the standalone Terminal-Bench 2.0 figure) -- none describe 61.6 as combining Terminal-Bench v1 into the v2 number. Confirmed false positive; both existing terminal-bench-2 entries (self-reported and independent) left unchanged, no relabeling needed.

FOURTH SUPPLEMENTARY PASS (deep second-pass search for mmlu/math/humaneval via non-obvious source types, 2026-07-17): The prior (third) pass already ruled out these three gaps via the obvious sources (official launch blog + mirror, HF sibling model cards, Artificial Analysis's own model page, vals.ai). This pass deliberately used six different source types instead, per an explicit research brief:

(1) Stanford HELM: crfm.stanford.edu/helm/mmlu/latest/ and crfm.stanford.edu/helm/classic/latest/ are both client-side-rendered React apps -- direct fetch returned only the page title, no leaderboard rows, consistent with the JS-dashboard problem already logged for Vals.ai/Epoch AI in this file. Cross-checked via web search and the stanford-crfm/helm GitHub repo (issues, CHANGELOG, LEADERBOARD_CHANGELOG references) for any mention of "Qwen3.6" -- found only generic Qwen1.5/Qwen2/Qwen3-series references (e.g. a GitHub issue about MedHELM + Qwen3 thinking-mode token extraction), nothing naming Qwen3.6-Plus specifically. Conclusion: HELM has not (yet) evaluated this model -- unsurprising, since HELM's evaluation cadence for a private/API-gated Alibaba Cloud model this recent (GA 2026-04-02) would require the HELM team to manually add API credentials for it, which apparently hasn't happened as of 2026-07-17.

(2) arXiv / Semantic Scholar: searched "Qwen3.6-Plus MMLU site:arxiv.org" and several variants. No arXiv paper mentions "Qwen3.6-Plus" as a baseline at all (the model is a very recent closed/proprietary API tier, not the kind of model academic papers tend to cite as a reproducible baseline within ~3.5 months of release). All arXiv hits were either about unrelated models (Qwen2.5-Plus, Qwen3-32B-Base) or Qwen's own technical reports for older/different models (Qwen3, Qwen3-Coder-Next, Qwen3.5-Omni) that predate or are siblings of, but do not include, this exact model.

(3) GitHub eval-harness run logs: ran `gh search code "qwen3.6-plus"` (30 hits) and `gh search repos "qwen3.6"` (30 hits). The code search surfaced dozens of real repos that reference the model as a routing/config string (provider configs, CLI switches, pricing tables) but zero committed lm-evaluation-harness results.json or benchmark-run logs scoring it on MMLU/MATH/HumanEval. One promising lead, jeinlee1991/chinese-llm-benchmark (a real, actively-maintained 中文AI大模型能力评测 / "ReLE" community eval harness with per-benchmark leaderboard/*.md result files and a combined LMArena+AA.md table), was fetched in full (README.md, table_leaderboard.md, LMArena+AA.md, 每周最新模型.md) via raw.githubusercontent.com -- qwen3.6-plus does NOT appear in any of its actual leaderboard/result tables (only a one-line prose announcement in the "weekly newest models" log), and the repo's own benchmark suite is Gaokao-exam/BFCL/CLUEWSC-based rather than MMLU/MATH/HumanEval, so even if the model were listed it would not have supplied these specific slugs.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators not in the first pass's checklist: fetched llm-stats.com's three dedicated per-benchmark leaderboard pages directly -- llm-stats.com/benchmarks/mmlu (100 models listed, no Qwen3.6-Plus row), llm-stats.com/benchmarks/math (71 models, no Qwen3.6-Plus row), llm-stats.com/benchmarks/humaneval (66 models, no Qwen3.6-Plus row) -- a materially different check than the first pass's visit to the model's own llm-stats.com page. Also re-fetched openrouter.ai/qwen/qwen3.6-plus/benchmarks directly: it exposes exactly one score (SWE-bench Verified 78.8, already in this file) and nothing for MMLU/MATH/HumanEval. Searched LiveBench.ai, Scale AI SEAL/labs.scale.com, and PapersWithCode for this model by name -- no model-specific hits for any of the three benchmarks on any of these sites.

(5) Non-English sources: searched Chinese ("Qwen3.6 Plus 千问3.6 MMLU 数学 跑分"), Japanese, and Korean queries. These surfaced numerous Chinese blog/news write-ups (datalearner.com, chinanews.com.cn, zhihu, csdn) but two of the most specific (zhihu, csdn) returned HTTP 403 / DNS-blocked to direct fetch, and the AI-summarized search results that did come through were internally inconsistent and not safely citable: one claims "Qwen3.6-Plus achieves 86.5% on MATH", sourced to DataLearnerAI's page titled "Qwen 3.6 Plus **Preview**" -- but a follow-up search confirmed "Qwen 3.6 Plus Preview" is a documented DISTINCT model from this corpus entry (released 2026-03-30, two days before GA, text-only/no-vision, free OpenRouter preview tier collecting prompt data) rather than the same GA "Qwen3.6-Plus" (multimodal, released 2026-04-02, priced $0.50/$3.00) -- exactly the variant-conflation risk this corpus's own audit already flags as its top systemic issue. Per that risk, this MATH=86.5 figure was NOT added to this file: it cannot be safely attributed to the GA Plus tier rather than the Preview tier, and no second source corroborated it for the GA model specifically. Other Japanese/Korean search summaries returned only already-known MMLU-Pro/MMLU-Redux figures mislabeled as plain "MMLU", or cited benchmark names ("MMAnswerBench" at 83.8%) that match names this file's own verificationNotes already flagged as suspicious/likely-padding benchlm.ai entries -- none of these cleared the bar for a credible, distinctly-sourced mmlu/math/humaneval number.

(6) Sibling models: this file's existing verificationNotes already record that both same-generation open-weight siblings (Qwen3.6-27B, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B) omit mmlu/math/humaneval from their own HF model cards. This pass additionally checked two Qwen3.7-generation comparison articles that explicitly reference Qwen3.6-Plus as a prior-gen baseline (atlascloud.ai's Qwen3.7-Plus-vs-Max benchmark writeup, yottalabs.ai's "Qwen 3.7 vs Qwen 3.6" writeup) -- neither contains an MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval column for the Qwen3.6-Plus row; the yottalabs piece's only Qwen3.6-Plus-specific data point is a partial, non-comparable AIME 2025 run (6 of 15 problems completed before the test was aborted for latency), not a MATH/MMLU/HumanEval score. The mindstudio.ai "Kimmy K2.6 and Qwen 3.6" comparison article (also sibling-family cross-reference) contains only a SWE-bench Verified table, no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval.

All 6 newly-specified source types were checked and none produced a credible, citable mmlu/math/humaneval score attributable to this exact GA model. Confirms the third pass's conclusion: this remains a genuine, expected absence given the Qwen3.6 line has moved to MMLU-Pro/MMLU-Redux, AIME/MathVision/HMMT, and LiveCodeBench/SWE-bench-family successors industry-wide, and no independent evaluator (HELM, an academic paper, a GitHub eval harness, a non-vendor leaderboard, or a non-English community benchmark) appears to have run this specific proprietary API model against the legacy MMLU/MATH/HumanEval suites as of 2026-07-17. Re-affirming omitted_no_evidence for all three slugs; no new results added this round.
